one_line_summary: DeepSeek V4 Flash: SM12x (GB10/RTX Pro 6000) support with hang fix and stability patches
technical_summary: Adds support for running DeepSeek V4 Flash on SM12x hardware (DGX Spark GB10/SM121 and RTX Pro 6000/SM120), including essential patches to prevent device hang on GB10 during model load and a stability fix that prevents crashes on 2×GB10 or 2×RTX Pro 6000 configurations in agentic use cases. Validated with TP=2, FP8 KV cache, and expert parallelism; GSM8K accuracy is 95.1% without MTP and 95.0% with 2-token MTP speculation. Full max-context (393K tokens) reliability also tested.

cross_references.why: Superseded PR for SM12x DeepSeek V4 Flash support

reasoning: The PR's primary purpose is enabling DeepSeek V4 Flash model support on a new hardware target (SM12x/GB10/RTX Pro 6000), with FP8 KV cache as a secondary aspect.

[None][test] Add MLA chunked-prefill SM dispatch regression coverage
one_line_summary: Regression test for SM90/SM100 MLA chunked-prefill dispatch gate in PyTorch backend
technical_summary: Adds a regression test (`test_mla_chunked_prefill_dispatch_by_sm`) for the SM-gated MLA chunked-prefill dispatch logic in TensorRT-LLM's PyTorch backend. The test uses monkeypatching (no GPU required) to assert that SM90 (Hopper) routes to `forward_context_with_cached_kv` while SM100+ routes to `forward_context_with_chunked_prefill`. This guards against accidental drift in the correctness safeguard that prevents inaccurate online-softmax merging on Hopper. No runtime behavior is changed.

cross_references.why: Upstream SM-gated MLA chunked-prefill mitigation this test covers

reasoning: The PR exclusively adds test coverage for MLA chunked-prefill attention dispatch behavior, placing it squarely in the attention category.

one_line_summary: Refactor vllm/envs.py to pydantic-settings, halving file size while preserving public API
technical_summary: Refactors `vllm/envs.py` from ~1984 lines to ~994 lines by migrating to `pydantic-settings`, replacing triple-declaration per env var (TYPE_CHECKING annotation + lambda dict + hand-rolled validators) with a single `Field` declaration per domain-specific `BaseSettings` sub-class. The public `vllm.envs.VLLM_X` attribute API is preserved, and `compile_factors()` output (load-bearing for torch.compile cache keys) is kept byte-for-byte identical. Lazy evaluation, `enable_envs_cache()` wrapping, and back-compat shims for `environment_variables` and `maybe_convert_bool` are all preserved.
reasoning: The PR is a code-quality refactor of the env-var configuration system with no kernel or model changes; the closest category is torch_compile since `compile_factors()` (torch.compile cache keys) is a load-bearing concern of the refactor, but this is primarily a chore/internal refactor.

one_line_summary: TRITON_MLA_SPARSE: Triton sparse MLA attention backend for SM80/SM121 (A100, GB10)
technical_summary: Introduces `TRITON_MLA_SPARSE` backend enabling sparse MLA (GLM-5, DeepSeek-V3.2) on SM80 (A100/A800) and SM121 (GB10/DGX Spark) where DeepGEMM/FlashMLA-Sparse/FlashInfer-MLA-Sparse are unavailable. Key components: a Triton `fp8_mqa_logits` kernel reproducing DeepGEMM prefill + paged MQA logits with a 256-entry bf16 LUT for FP8 decode, a split-KV decode kernel with N-way online-softmax merge, CUDA graph support via `UNIFORM_BATCH`, a fix for a persistent topk race condition (`cudaMemsetAsync` before each launch), and MXFP4 link stubs for SM8.x builds. Benchmarks on 8×A100 SXM TP=8 show TPOT median of 19.3 ms (short context, 1744 tokens) and 22.9 ms (long context, 127744 tokens) with total throughput of 429.5–700.1 tok/s.
reasoning: The PR's primary contribution is a new Triton-based sparse MLA attention backend (`TRITON_MLA_SPARSE`) targeting SM80/SM121 hardware where existing MLA-sparse backends are unavailable.

dist-ckpt: add local-replica mode to eliminate cross-rank reads of replicated params on torch_dist load
one_line_summary: dist-ckpt: local-replica mode eliminates cross-rank reads of replicated params at large scale
technical_summary: Adds a 'local replica' mode to torch_dist checkpoints that eliminates cross-rank reads of replicated parameters (RMSNorms, MoE router weights, embeddings, optimizer-state mirrors) at large scale. Each rank that would cross-read writes a shadow copy (`__<rank>_*.distcp`) during save; at load time a redirect maps reads to the local file, avoiding filesystem metadata service collapse with thousands of ranks. Two new config knobs (`--ckpt-fully-parallel-save-replicate-local` / `--ckpt-fully-parallel-load-replicate-local`) are decoupled so save and load topology can differ. Shadow keys use a `__shadow_<global_rank>__<fqn>` prefix to avoid PyT DCP's MetadataIndex dedup collapsing them.
reasoning: The PR's core contribution is a distributed checkpointing optimization that eliminates cross-rank filesystem reads on replicated tensors, directly improving scalability of the torch_dist checkpoint backend.

Muon CPU offload
one_line_summary: Muon optimizer CPU offload for FP32 master weights and optimizer states
technical_summary: Adds CPU offloading for the Muon optimizer, moving FP32 master weights and optimizer states to CPU after each optimizer step and reloading them before the next step. This reduces peak GPU memory during forward/backward passes (but not during the optimizer step itself), unblocking OOM issues observed in GLM5 training. Unit tests are included and validated against GLM5 training runs.

cross_references.why: Linked issue tracking OOM problem that this PR resolves

reasoning: The PR explicitly implements CPU offload for the Muon optimizer's master weights and optimizer states, making optimizer the primary category with activation offload as a secondary concern.

[Scheduler] Priority scheduling supports preemption of requests in the running queue by requests in the waiting queue
one_line_summary: v1 scheduler: priority preemption of running requests by waiting-queue requests
technical_summary: Extends v1 priority scheduling to support preemption of running-queue requests by higher-priority requests in the waiting queue, fixing issue #40004. Previously, priority scheduling only applied at admission time; now a waiting request with higher priority can preempt a currently running request when the scheduler is at `max_num_seqs`. A new test `test_priority_preemption_at_max_num_seqs` was added to `tests/v1/core/test_scheduler.py`.
reasoning: The PR directly modifies the v1 scheduler to add priority-based preemption logic, which is core scheduling policy.

[Main][feat] Support CUDA Graph capture offloading modules
one_line_summary: Fine-Grained Activation Offloading + TE CUDA Graph capture/replay co-enablement
technical_summary: Enables Fine-Grained Activation Offloading to interoperate with Transformer Engine CUDA Graph capture and replay, which were previously mutually exclusive. Introduces dedicated CUDA stream/event synchronization (`cuda_graph_stream`/`cuda_graph_event`) in `PipelineOffloadManager`, a deferred-commit mode (`delay_offload_until_cuda_graph`) that batches D2H copies into CPU-idle windows between graph launch and communication, warmup hooks to disable offloading during TE's eager warmup phase, and automatic detection of which modules can be offloaded within a captured sub-graph. New config options include `activation_offload_fraction` and `delta_offload_bytes_across_pp_ranks`; requires TE >= 2.14 and `cuda_graph_impl="transformer_engine"`.
reasoning: The PR's central contribution is making fine-grained activation offloading work inside CUDA graph capture/replay, placing it firmly in activation_offload with torch.compile/graph as a strong secondary.

[Feature] Add /live liveness probe, LLM.shutdown(), and k8s shutdown docs
one_line_summary: /live liveness probe, LLM.shutdown(), and Kubernetes graceful shutdown docs
technical_summary: Adds a `/live` liveness probe endpoint that returns 200 during graceful shutdown/drain and 503 only on fatal engine error, allowing Kubernetes to distinguish pod health from drain state. Introduces `LLM.shutdown(timeout)` for programmatic shutdown, adds `is_engine_dead` to the EngineClient protocol, exempts `/live` and `/metrics` from ScalingMiddleware, and updates k8s deployment docs with a full Deployment YAML including `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` and optional preStop hook. Implements follow-up items from RFC #24885.

cross_references.why: RFC on shutdown semantics this PR implements

reasoning: This PR is about serving infrastructure: liveness/readiness probe semantics, graceful shutdown APIs, and Kubernetes deployment documentation — all squarely in the serving/scheduler category.

title: Fix Megatron-FSDP optimizer CPU offload and checkpointing
one_line_summary: Fix optimizer CPU offload for Megatron-FSDP DTensor parameters and gradients
technical_summary: Fixes CPU offload support for optimizer states when using Megatron-FSDP with DTensor parameters. The fix operates on local shards of DTensor parameters/gradients before the CPU offload copy, and avoids incorrectly dispatching `pin_memory`/`is_pinned` through the DTensor abstraction while respecting the `pin_cpu_params` flag. Verified with unit tests in `test_optimizer_cpu_offloading.py`.

one_line_summary: KV cache events: preserve original cache_salt string alongside hashed cache_salt_id
technical_summary: Threads the original `cache_salt` string (previously discarded after hashing to `uint64 cache_salt_id`) through the full pipeline from Python LLM API → C++ Request → LlmRequest → BlockKey → KVCacheStoredBlockData → nanobind → Python serializer. This allows external cache managers to identify tenant ownership of cached blocks in KV cache stored-block events without a side-channel mapping, following the pattern of multimodal UUIDs (`MmKey.uuid`). The string is carried alongside the existing `cacheSaltID` and does not affect hashing or matching logic.
reasoning: The PR explicitly modifies KV cache block data structures and event serialization to propagate cache_salt strings through the KV cache management pipeline.
